theory.intro: When an expression has several operations, everyone agrees to work them out in a fixed order so the answer is the same.
facts: Do **brackets** first, then **multiplication and division** (left to right), then **addition and subtraction** (left to right).
Multiplication and division have **equal** priority — work left to right.
Without the agreed order, the same expression could give different answers.
steps: Do all $\times$ and $\div$ from left to right.
Do all $+$ and $-$ from left to right.
